name: Academic Writer Setup
description: First-time setup: researcher profile, tool detection, style fingerprint analysis, source indexing.

nodes.prompt: Detect available integrations: check if ck CLI exists (Candlekeep), if Agentic-Search-Vectorless is reachable at localhost:8000, if cognetivy CLI exists, if MongoDB Agent Skills MCP is configured. Output each tool with enabled/disabled status and path.
Ask the researcher: (1) Field of study, (2) Target language (default: Hebrew), (3) Citation style (default: inline-parenthetical for Hebrew). Collect answers and store.

Read all files in past-articles/ folder (PDF/DOCX). Analyze writing patterns across 25 dimensions: sentence length, structure variety, openers, passive voice, vocabulary complexity, register, paragraph structure, argument progression, evidence handling, tone, transitions, citation integration, rhetorical patterns. Extract representative excerpts.
Present the auto-detected style fingerprint to the researcher. Ask if they want to adjust any dimension, add preferred transitions, or provide additional style context.

Combine all inputs into the final profile.md: fieldOfStudy, targetLanguage, citationStyle, outputFormatPreferences, styleFingerprint (25 dimensions + representativeExcerpts), tools config, articleStructure conventions. Write to .academic-helper/profile.md in Markdown frontmatter + JSON sections format.
If Candlekeep is enabled: list all items via ck items list, store in profile.sources array. If Agentic-Search-Vectorless is enabled: ingest Candlekeep items into vectorless. Output the source index.
